ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF AND MOTION DEADLINE. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 36(A). THE NEW TRIAL DATE IS FEBRUARY 8, 2021 AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36(F) IS FEBRUARY 19, 2021. LOCAL RULE 20 SHALL BE FOLLOWED BY THE PARTIES AS WELL AS ALL SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULES AND CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS FEBRUARY 4, 2021. A CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR DECEMBER 10, 2020 AT 1:30 P.M. NO NEED FOR ANY PARTY TO FILE ANY CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE STATEMENT. PARTIES MAY APPEAR AT THE CONFERENCE BY TELEPHONE. IF THE PARTIES AGREE THAT NO CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS NECESSARY, THEY SHALL NOTIFY THE COURT AT LEAST TWO DAY PRIOR TO TAKE THE CMC OFF CALENDAR. MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 4, 2021 AT 9 A.M.. IN 603. PERSONAL APPEARANCE IS REQUIRED BY ALL UNLESS A PARTY IS GRANTED EXCUSE BASED ON GOOD CAUSE. ORDER SIGNED. Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ** Mini-Minutes ** Part 1 of 2 **
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF AND MOTION DEADLINE

Appearances via Court Call: Sandra L. Gryder of Pond North LLP (213.317.6170) for Baker Distributing, LLC; Robert Bugatto of Vasquez Estrada & Conway LLP (415.378.5158) for SPX Cooling Technologies; Richard Lee of Selman Breitman LLP (415.979.0400) for Douglass Insulation Company, Inc. and Ray L. Hellwig Plumbing & Heating; Jonathan Kim of Foley & Mansfield PLLP (510.590.9500) for RF MacDonald Co.; Jamie O. Norman of Litchfield Cavo LLP (626.683.1100) for Baltimore Aircoil Company, Inc.; Gilliam Stewart of CMBG3 Law LLC (949.467.9503) for Armstrong International.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). Oppositions filed by SPX Cooling Technologies Inc., R.F. MacDonald Co. with joinder by Armstrong International, Baker Distributing Company LLC. Discovery report filed by Designated Defense Counsel. The Court did not consider the opposition filed by Baltimore Aircoil Company for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7B.)

The trial date of January 25, 2021 is vacated. The new trial date is February 8, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is February 19, 2021. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is February 4, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is January 22, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date is February 5, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than November 20, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
** End of Part 1 ** Ruling continued in part 2 **

Plaintiffs' Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial; And Extending Discovery Cutoff And Motion Deadline
Off Calendar Oct-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
** Mini-Minutes ** Part 2 of 2 **
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF AND MOTION DEADLINE

No later than November 5, 2020, plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than November 5, 2020, defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for December 10, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than December 25, 2020. Responses to the demand are due no later than January 8, 2021. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by January 29, 2021 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, February 4, 2021 at 9 a.m.. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed. Judge: Cynthia M. Lee, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter (via Court Call): Jennifer Roux, CSR 11033 (T: 510.451.1580, E: jenrouxcsr@gmail.com) (503/CML)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-24-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Dann DeMund of THE SIEVING LAW FIRM, APC (916-444-3366), appearing through CourtCall for defendant and moving party Jeld-Wen, Inc.
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Stanley Bandy and Hendrika Bandy.

RULING: DEFENDANT JELD-WEN, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Jeld-Wen, Inc.'s Demurrer to Plaintiffs Complaint is overruled on the following grounds: (1) Jeld-Wen failed to comply with California Rules of Court, rule 3.1320(a). The attempt to comply in Jeld-Wen's reply does not cure the defect. (2) The demurrer is overruled on its merits. Jeld-Wen's demurrer to the first cause of action for negligence is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a cause of action for negligence. (See Complaint 1-20.) Jeld-Wen's demurrer to the second cause of action for products liability is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a cause of action for products liability. (See Complaint 3-4, 21-36.) Jeld-Wen's demurrer to the eighth cause of action for loss of consortium is overruled for similar reasons. (See Complaint 128-132.) (3) As to Jeld-Wen's assertion that the 1997 bankruptcy order bars plaintiff's claim, the determination is highly factual and not appropriate for demurrer. (See Flores v. Kmart Corp. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1316; see In re Zilog, Inc. (9th Cir.) 450 F.3d 996; see In re Grossman's Inc. (3rd. Cir. 2010) 607 F.3d 114.) Accordingly, pursuant to Evidence Code section 452(d), the Court declines to take judicial notice of the 1997 bankruptcy order because it is irrelevant. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
